title: Kardomia prominens
taxonomy: Kardomia prominens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It belongs to the genus Kardomia, which is a group of plants endemic to the Pacific Islands. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The family Apiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many edible and medicinal species. Kardomia prominens is a relatively rare and poorly studied species, and as such, its classification may be subject to revision as more research becomes available.

intro: Kardomia prominens, also known as the Australian black sallet, is a small shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving pain and reducing the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Kardomia prominens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
